content: The 2024–2025 SOTA push for structured, retrieval-augmented active memory in interactive agents can be reframed as an evolving internal "curiosity filter" that prioritizes storing only the uncertain, environment-discovering information that aligns with an agent’s intrinsic exploration goals, rather than uniformly caching all observed data. This reframing redefines cutting-edge active agent memory not as a passive storage system, but as a core component of curiosity-driven exploration itself.
